title: What Is Retatrutide (GLP-3)? The Triple Agonist Obesity Drug Explained
h2_questions: What is retatrutide?
Why is retatrutide called GLP-3?
Is there a GLP-3 pill?
What makes retatrutide a triple agonist?
Who is developing retatrutide?
How does the GGG tri-agonist mechanism work?
key_data_points: Retatrutide is an investigational once-weekly injectable drug developed by Eli Lilly
It is a triple agonist: activates GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors simultaneously
Informally called 'GLP-3' in consumer media; this is not an official pharmacological classification
No oral (pill) form of retatrutide is currently in development or approved
Retatrutide is not the same molecule as GLP-1; the 'GLP-3' nickname is colloquial
INN (International Nonproprietary Name) is retatrutide; Eli Lilly has not announced a brand name

title: Retatrutide vs. Tirzepatide vs. Semaglutide: How the Weight Loss Numbers Stack Up
secondary_keywords: triple agonist vs dual agonist weight loss
h2_questions: How much weight loss does retatrutide produce compared to tirzepatide and semaglutide?

key_data_points: Retatrutide 12mg: 24.2% mean weight loss at 48 weeks (Phase 2) and 28.7% at 68 weeks (TRIUMPH-4 Phase 3)
Retatrutide 8mg: 100% of participants achieved ≥5% weight loss at 48 weeks
Retatrutide 12mg: 48% of participants achieved ≥25% weight loss at 48 weeks in Phase 2
TRIUMPH-4 28.7% mean weight loss is the highest ever recorded in a Phase 3 obesity trial
Retatrutide targets GLP-1, GIP, and glucagon receptors vs tirzepatide (GLP-1 + GIP) and semaglutide (GLP-1 only)
Dysesthesia at 12mg retatrutide: 20.9% in TRIUMPH-4 vs 0.7% placebo — a side effect not prominently seen with tirzepatide or semaglutide
NDA for retatrutide not yet filed as of April 2026; tirzepatide and semaglutide already FDA-approved for obesity

title: Retatrutide Patient Eligibility: Who Qualifies Based on Phase 2 and Phase 3 Trial Criteria?
h2_questions: What BMI do you need to qualify for retatrutide based on clinical trial criteria?
Which medical conditions were required or excluded in the retatrutide trials?
Can people with type 2 diabetes use retatrutide?
Does prior use of semaglutide or tirzepatide disqualify you from retatrutide treatment?
key_data_points: Phase 2 eligibility: BMI ≥30 kg/m² (up to ≤50) or ≥27 with comorbidity such as hypertension or dyslipidemia
Exclusion: type 2 diabetes defined as HbA1c ≥6.5%, fasting glucose ≥126 mg/dL, or random glucose ≥200 mg/dL
Exclusion: prior bariatric surgery (except liposuction), prior GLP-1RA use, body weight change >5kg in prior 3 months
All participants received diet and physical activity counseling as background therapy
TRIUMPH-4 Phase 3 trial: 68-week duration; 12mg dose achieved 28.7% mean weight loss
Phase 3 publication and NDA filing both pending as of April 2026

title: Retatrutide Tolerability in Phase 3: What TRIUMPH-4 Discontinuation Rates and Dysesthesia Data Mean for Patients
h2_questions: How do retatrutide discontinuation rates in TRIUMPH-4 compare to Phase 2 trial data?
What is dysesthesia and why does it occur more frequently at the 12mg retatrutide dose?
Did any TRIUMPH-4 participants discontinue because of excessive weight loss — and what does that tell us?
How should patients and physicians weigh the tolerability profile of retatrutide when choosing a dose?
key_data_points: TRIUMPH-4 Phase 3: discontinuation rates 12.2% (9mg) and 18.2% (12mg) vs 4.0% placebo at 68 weeks
TRIUMPH-4 dysesthesia: 8.8% at 9mg, 20.9% at 12mg vs 0.7% placebo
Phase 2 hyperesthesia/dysesthesia: 12.9% at 12mg and 14.3% at 8mg [ID 4mg] vs 1.4% placebo (Tables S14)
TRIUMPH-4 titration schedule: 2mg → 4mg → 6mg → 9mg → 12mg maintenance starting week 17
Some TRIUMPH-4 discontinuations attributed to perceived excessive weight loss — a novel finding in obesity pharmacotherapy
Phase 2 most common AEs: nausea, diarrhea, vomiting — GI events dose-dependent and peaked during titration
Mean weight loss at 12mg in TRIUMPH-4: 28.7% — highest recorded in any Phase 3 obesity trial
Phase 3 peer-reviewed publication and NDA filing both pending as of April 2026
